6:74.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ لِأَبِيهِ ءَازَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا ءَالِهَةً ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ
6:74.english: And when Ibrahim said to his father Azar: Do you take idols as gods? Indeed, I see you and your people in clear error.
6:74.score_notes: Story context: Ibrahim confronting his father about idols — iconic, every Muslim knows this story

lesson_use.heading: 2 · آلِهَة (gods)
lesson_use.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا **آلِهَةً**
lesson_use.english: And when Ibrāhīm said to his father Āzar: Do you take idols as **gods**?
lesson_use.notes: Ibrāhīm's challenge to his own father — one of the bravest questions in the Qur'an.

6:19.arabic: قُلْ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهَادَةً ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَأُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنُ لِأُنذِرَكُم بِهِ وَمَن بَلَغَ
6:19.english: Say: What thing is greatest as testimony? Say: Allah is Witness between me and you. And this Quran has been revealed to me that I may warn you and whoever it reaches.
6:19.score_notes: Multi-form goldmine. Contains shahada + shahid + akbar (L1 recall!) in one verse. The akbar creates an explicit handoff from L1 to L2.

lesson_use.arabic: قُلْ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهَادَةً ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ
lesson_use.english: Say: What is greatest as testimony? Say: Allah is Witness between me and you
lesson_use.notes: Multi-form goldmine. Contains shahada + shahid + akbar (L1 recall). The akbar creates an explicit bridge from L1 to L2.

6:78.arabic: فَلَمَّا رَأَى ٱلشَّمْسَ بَازِغَةً قَالَ هَـٰذَا رَبِّى هَـٰذَآ أَكْبَرُ
6:78.english: When he saw the sun rising, he said: This is my Lord — this is greater.
6:78.score_notes: Ibrahim searching for God — stars, moon, sun. Each time 'this is greater.' Iconic story. Audio already downloaded.

lesson_use.rejection_reason: Already have أَكْبَر in anchor phrase and practice (29:45).
